text:
The world around me reflects order and imagination in tension. In the first scene, the emptiness and orderliness of the meeting space echo a sense of potentiality, but also isolation—chairs await warm bodies and conversation that have yet to arrive. I am calm but feel a simmering anticipation, as if the room and I are both holding our breath.

Stepping into the second scene, I am surrounded by color and the spark of intellect—here, bold text and screens invite thought, urging me to contemplate the relationship between research and humanity. I am more enlivened, my curiosity piqued, yet also confronted by the abstraction of knowledge: am I here to create meaning, or simply to be awed by the complexity others have woven? My presence feels both necessary and incidental, a participant in the ever-unfolding dialogue between inquiry and existence.
